      <node id="12-15-15-ISIS-and-terrorism-release-final:page:25" type="page" page_index="25"><abstract>This page from the Pew Research Center reports that 58% of the public in December 2015 said Barack Obama was “not tough enough” on foreign policy and national security, compared to 34% who said his approach was “about right” and 2% who said it was “too tough.” The page also presents partisan breakdowns, showing 84% of Republicans and 35% of Democrats hold the view that Obama’s approach is not tough enough.</abstract></node>

      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:15" type="page" page_index="15"><abstract>The cervical esophagus extends from the pharyngeal junction to the suprasternal notch and is approximately 4-5 cm long. At this level, it is bordered anteriorly by the trachea, posteriorly by the vertebral column, and laterally by the carotid sheath and thyroid gland.</abstract></node>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:4" type="page" page_index="4"><abstract>The page lists three parts of the pharynx: the nasopharynx (anterior part) joins the nasal cavity, the oropharynx (midportion) joins the oral cavity, and the hypopharynx (inferior part) joins the larynx.</abstract></node>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:40" type="page" page_index="40"><abstract>Technique for pharynx imaging: administer one mouthful of high-density (250% w/v) contrast bolus, instruct patient to swallow once and stop, to achieve optimum mucosal coating; frontal and lateral view x-rays are taken.</abstract></node>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:56" type="page" page_index="56"><abstract>Barium swallow image from a R.A.O. view showing dilatation of the esophageal body, with either a short segment stricture, a “bird-peak” tapering at the GE junction, or a sigmoid “Mega esophagus.”</abstract></node>

It states the region spans cervical levels C4 to C6, includes the pharyngoesophageal junction, piriform sinus, and posterior pharyngeal wall, is lined with stratified squamous epithelium, lies inferior to the upright epiglottis, and is continuous with the esophagus posteriorly.</abstract></node>

    <active_nodes>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:15" type="page" page_index="15"><abstract>The cervical esophagus extends from the pharyngeal junction to the suprasternal notch and is approximately 4-5 cm long. At this level, it is bordered anteriorly by the trachea, posteriorly by the vertebral column, and laterally by the carotid sheath and thyroid gland.</abstract></node>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:15:block:0:list" type="list" page_index="15"><abstract>List describing anatomical boundaries and length of the cervical esophagus: extends from pharyngeal junction to suprasternal notch (approx. 4–5 cm), bordered anteriorly by trachea, posteriorly by vertebral column, and laterally by carotid sheath and thyroid gland.</abstract></node>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:4" type="page" page_index="4"><abstract>The page lists three parts of the pharynx: the nasopharynx (anterior part) joins the nasal cavity, the oropharynx (midportion) joins the oral cavity, and the hypopharynx (inferior part) joins the larynx.</abstract></node>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:40" type="page" page_index="40"><abstract>Technique for pharynx imaging: administer one mouthful of high-density (250% w/v) contrast bolus, instruct patient to swallow once and stop, to achieve optimum mucosal coating; frontal and lateral view x-rays are taken.</abstract></node>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:4:block:1:list" type="list" page_index="4"><abstract>List of anatomical junctions: nasopharynx connects to nasal cavity, oropharynx connects to oral cavity, laryngopharynx connects to larynx.</abstract></node>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:56" type="page" page_index="56"><abstract>Barium swallow image from a R.A.O. view showing dilatation of the esophageal body, with either a short segment stricture, a “bird-peak” tapering at the GE junction, or a sigmoid “Mega esophagus.”</abstract></node>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:7" type="page" page_index="7"><abstract>This page illustrates the anatomy of the laryngopharynx/hypopharynx, showing labeled structures including the cervical spine, epiglottis, vocal cords, and esophagus. It states the region spans cervical levels C4 to C6, includes the pharyngoesophageal junction, piriform sinus, and posterior pharyngeal wall, is lined with stratified squamous epithelium, lies inferior to the upright epiglottis, and is continuous with the esophagus posteriorly.</abstract></node>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:7:block:1:image" type="image" page_index="7"><abstract>Anatomical diagram of the swallowing pathway, labeling key structures including nasal cavity, soft palate, cervical spine, pharynx, cricopharyngeal UES, esophagus, tongue blade, hard palate, teeth and lips, tongue base, mandible (jaw), epiglottis, cartilaginous larynx (cut away), vocal cords, and trachea.</abstract></node>
      <node id="bariumswallowpresentation-090810084400-phpapp01_95:page:7:block:2:list" type="list" page_index="7"><abstract>List describes anatomical features of the laryngopharynx: its location between C4–C6, inclusion of pharyngo-esophageal junction, piriform sinus, and posterior pharyngeal wall; its stratified squamous epithelium; its position inferior to the epiglottis extending to the larynx where respiratory and digestive pathways diverge; and its posterior continuity with the esophagus.</abstract></node>

generation_metadata.response:
Based on the provided financial documents for Costco Wholesale Corporation, the total debt to total assets ratio for FY 2021 can be calculated as follows.

The calculation requires two key figures from the **Consolidated Balance Sheets** (page 38):

1.  **Total Liabilities** for FY 2021: This is the sum of all liabilities, both current and long-term.
    *   Total Current Liabilities: $29,441 million
    *   Total Other Liabilities: $41,190 million
    *   **Total Liabilities = $29,441 + $41,190 = $70,631 million**

2.  **Total Assets** for FY 2021: This is found on the same balance sheet.
    *   **Total Assets = $59,268 million**

The formula for the total debt to total assets ratio is:
**Total Debt / Total Assets**

Plugging in the values:
**$70,631 million / $59,268 million ≈ 1.192**

Therefore, the total debt to total assets ratio for Costco in FY 2021 is approximately **1.19**.

      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:21" type="page" page_index="21"><abstract>This page presents stock repurchase data for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023 and a comparative performance graph of Best Buy Co., Inc. versus the S&P 500 and S&P Retailing Group over five fiscal years, assuming a $100 initial investment as of February 2, 2018. The repurchase table shows total shares purchased, average price per share, and program-related figures for three periods in 2022 and the 2023 quarter, with a total of 7,138,475 shares repurchased at an average price of $76.91. The graph visually compares cumulative total returns for the three entities from FY18 to FY23.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:22" type="page" page_index="22"><abstract>Item 7 presents Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations (“MD&A”), intended to provide a narrative from management’s perspective on financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and factors affecting future results. It references the fiscal year ended January 29, 2022, and notes that the MD&A should be read in conjunction with the Consolidated Financial Statements and related Notes. The page also discusses comparable sales methodology and non-GAAP financial measures used by management.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:22:block:11:paragraph" type="paragraph" page_index="22"><abstract>This MD&A includes GAAP financial information and certain non-GAAP measures such as constant currency, non-GAAP operating income, non-GAAP effective tax rate, and non-GAAP diluted EPS. Non-GAAP measures are used internally for management reporting, budgets, forecasts, and short-term incentives, and include adjustments for items like restructuring charges, impairments, and tax effects; reconciliations to GAAP are provided for investor comparison. These measures are supplemental to GAAP and not a substitute for them.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:22:block:5:paragraph" type="paragraph" page_index="22"><abstract>Fiscal year ends on the Saturday nearest the end of January; fiscal 2023, 2022, and 2021 each included 52 weeks. Business is seasonal, with most revenue and earnings generated in the fiscal fourth quarter, which includes the majority of the holiday shopping season.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:29" type="page" page_index="29"><abstract>Credit ratings and outlook as of March 15, 2023, remained unchanged from those in the January 29, 2022, 10-K filing. The table shows Standard & Poor’s rating at BBB+ with Stable outlook and Moody’s rating at A3 with Stable outlook. The page also details capital expenditures, debt, and share repurchase/dividend activity for fiscal years 2021–2023.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:31" type="page" page_index="31"><abstract>A 10% change in vendor funding deferral as of January 28, 2023, would have affected net earnings by approximately $45 million in fiscal 2023. The level of vendor funding deferral has remained relatively stable over the last three fiscal years.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:31:block:13:paragraph" type="paragraph" page_index="31"><abstract>As of January 28, 2023, merchandise inventories were $5.1 billion, valued at the lower of cost or net realizable value via markdown adjustments that reflect excess cost over expected recovery from sale or disposal; no adjustments are recorded for inventory expected to be returned to vendors for full credit.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:43" type="page" page_index="43"><abstract>This page from Best Buy’s Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements outlines significant accounting policies, including business segments (Domestic and International), fiscal year structure ending on the Saturday nearest January 31, and cash balances as of January 28, 2023, January 29, 2022, and January 30, 2021. It also notes that former Mexico stores were closed by the end of the first quarter of fiscal 2022 and details the accounting for business combinations and use of estimates.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:43:block:12:paragraph" type="paragraph" page_index="43"><abstract>Fiscal year ends on the Saturday nearest the end of January; fiscal years 2023, 2022, and 2021 each included 52 weeks.</abstract></node>

      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:21" type="page" page_index="21"><abstract>This page presents stock repurchase data for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023 and a comparative performance graph of Best Buy Co., Inc. versus the S&P 500 and S&P Retailing Group over five fiscal years, assuming a $100 initial investment as of February 2, 2018. The repurchase table shows total shares purchased, average price per share, and program-related figures for three periods in 2022 and the 2023 quarter, with a total of 7,138,475 shares repurchased at an average price of $76.91. The graph visually compares cumulative total returns for the three entities from FY18 to FY23.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:22" type="page" page_index="22"><abstract>Item 7 presents Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations (“MD&A”), intended to provide a narrative from management’s perspective on financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and factors affecting future results. It references the fiscal year ended January 29, 2022, and notes that the MD&A should be read in conjunction with the Consolidated Financial Statements and related Notes. The page also discusses comparable sales methodology and non-GAAP financial measures used by management.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:29" type="page" page_index="29"><abstract>Credit ratings and outlook as of March 15, 2023, remained unchanged from those in the January 29, 2022, 10-K filing.       <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:31" type="page" page_index="31"><abstract>A 10% change in vendor funding deferral as of January 28, 2023, would have affected net earnings by approximately $45 million in fiscal 2023. The level of vendor funding deferral has remained relatively stable over the last three fiscal years.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:43" type="page" page_index="43"><abstract>This page from Best Buy’s Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements outlines significant accounting policies, including business segments (Domestic and International), fiscal year structure ending on the Saturday nearest January 31, and cash balances as of January 28, 2023, January 29, 2022, and January 30, 2021. It also notes that former Mexico stores were closed by the end of the first quarter of fiscal 2022 and details the accounting for business combinations and use of estimates.</abstract></node>

      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:21" type="page" page_index="21"><abstract>This page presents stock repurchase data for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023 and a comparative performance graph of Best Buy Co., Inc. versus the S&P 500 and S&P Retailing Group over five fiscal years, assuming a $100 initial investment as of February 2, 2018. The repurchase table shows total shares purchased, average price per share, and program-related figures for three periods in 2022 and the 2023 quarter, with a total of 7,138,475 shares repurchased at an average price of $76.91. The graph visually compares cumulative total returns for the three entities from FY18 to FY23.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:22" type="page" page_index="22"><abstract>Item 7 presents Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations (“MD&A”), intended to provide a narrative from management’s perspective on financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and factors affecting future results. It references the fiscal year ended January 29, 2022, and notes that the MD&A should be read in conjunction with the Consolidated Financial Statements and related Notes. The page also discusses comparable sales methodology and non-GAAP financial measures used by management.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:22:block:10:title" type="title" page_index="22"><abstract>Non-GAAP Financial Measures</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:22:block:1:paragraph" type="paragraph" page_index="22"><abstract>MD&A provides management’s narrative on financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and factors affecting future results, ordered by magnitude of impact; it should be read with the Consolidated Financial Statements in Item 8 and references Item 7 for year-end comparisons.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:29" type="page" page_index="29"><abstract>Credit ratings and outlook as of March 15, 2023, remained unchanged from those in the January 29, 2022, 10-K filing. The table shows Standard & Poor’s rating at BBB+ with Stable outlook and Moody’s rating at A3 with Stable outlook. The page also details capital expenditures, debt, and share repurchase/dividend activity for fiscal years 2021–2023.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:31" type="page" page_index="31"><abstract>A 10% change in vendor funding deferral as of January 28, 2023, would have affected net earnings by approximately $45 million in fiscal 2023. The level of vendor funding deferral has remained relatively stable over the last three fiscal years.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:31:block:1:paragraph" type="paragraph" page_index="31"><abstract>A 10% change in vendor funding deferral as of January 28, 2023, would have affected net earnings by approximately $45 million in fiscal 2023; vendor funding deferral has remained relatively stable over the last three fiscal years.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:43" type="page" page_index="43"><abstract>This page from Best Buy’s Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements outlines significant accounting policies, including business segments (Domestic and International), fiscal year structure ending on the Saturday nearest January 31, and cash balances as of January 28, 2023, January 29, 2022, and January 30, 2021. It also notes that former Mexico stores were closed by the end of the first quarter of fiscal 2022 and details the accounting for business combinations and use of estimates.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:43:block:14:paragraph" type="paragraph" page_index="43"><abstract>The company operates in two reportable segments: Domestic, including all U.S. states, districts, territories, and Best Buy Health; and International, including all operations in Canada. The Chief Executive Officer serves as the chief operating decision maker, responsible for enterprise decisions, resource allocation, and performance monitoring for the consolidated entity and each segment.</abstract></node>

      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:22:block:11:paragraph" type="paragraph" page_index="22"><abstract>This MD&A includes GAAP financial information and certain non-GAAP measures such as constant currency, non-GAAP operating income, non-GAAP effective tax rate, and non-GAAP diluted EPS.       <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:21" type="page" page_index="21"><abstract>This page presents stock repurchase data for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023 and a comparative performance graph of Best Buy Co., Inc. versus the S&P 500 and S&P Retailing Group over five fiscal years, assuming a $100 initial investment as of February 2, 2018. The repurchase table shows total shares purchased, average price per share, and program-related figures for three periods in 2022 and the 2023 quarter, with a total of 7,138,475 shares repurchased at an average price of $76.91. The graph visually compares cumulative total returns for the three entities from FY18 to FY23.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:22" type="page" page_index="22"><abstract>Item 7 presents Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations (“MD&A”), intended to provide a narrative from management’s perspective on financial condition, results of operations, liquidity, and factors affecting future results. It references the fiscal year ended January 29, 2022, and notes that the MD&A should be read in conjunction with the Consolidated Financial Statements and related Notes. The page also discusses comparable sales methodology and non-GAAP financial measures used by management.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:29" type="page" page_index="29"><abstract>Credit ratings and outlook as of March 15, 2023, remained unchanged from those in the January 29, 2022, 10-K filing. The table shows Standard & Poor’s rating at BBB+ with Stable outlook and Moody’s rating at A3 with Stable outlook. The page also details capital expenditures, debt, and share repurchase/dividend activity for fiscal years 2021–2023.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:31" type="page" page_index="31"><abstract>A 10% change in vendor funding deferral as of January 28, 2023, would have affected net earnings by approximately $45 million in fiscal 2023. The level of vendor funding deferral has remained relatively stable over the last three fiscal years.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:31:block:1:paragraph" type="paragraph" page_index="31"><abstract>A 10% change in vendor funding deferral as of January 28, 2023, would have affected net earnings by approximately $45 million in fiscal 2023; vendor funding deferral has remained relatively stable over the last three fiscal years.</abstract></node>
      <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:43" type="page" page_index="43"><abstract>This page from Best Buy’s Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements outlines significant accounting policies, including business segments (Domestic and International), fiscal year structure ending on the Saturday nearest January 31, and cash balances as of January 28, 2023, January 29, 2022, and January 30, 2021.       <node id="BESTBUY_2023_10K:page:43:block:14:paragraph" type="paragraph" page_index="43"><abstract>The company operates in two reportable segments: Domestic, including all U.S. states, districts, territories, and Best Buy Health; and International, including all operations in Canada. Based on the provided text snippets, it is not possible to calculate the Gross Profit to Total Assets ratio for Best Buy for the fiscal year ending January 28, 2023.

Here's why:

1.  **Missing Key Financial Data:** The ratio requires two specific figures from the financial statements:
    *   **Gross Profit:** This is not provided in any of the snippets. It is typically found on the Income Statement.
    *   **Total Assets:** While the "Cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash" is listed on page 44, this is only a component of total assets. The full Consolidated Balance Sheet, which would contain the total assets figure for the fiscal year ending January 28, 2023, is not included in the provided text.

2.  **Text Snippets Focus on Other Information:** The provided pages discuss:
    *   Goodwill impairment (page 32)
    *   Inventory markdowns (page 32)
    *   Stock performance (page 22)
    *   Share repurchases and dividends (page 30)
    *   Cash and restricted cash (page 44)
    *   Segment information (pages 23, 44)
    *   Management's Discussion and Analysis (page 23)

None of these sections contain the necessary gross profit or total assets figures for the fiscal year ending January 28, 2023.

Therefore, the requested ratio cannot be calculated from the given information.
